subject: maze
hookText: Hand them this. Watch what happens.

pinterest.text: Most 6-year-olds take 4 tries to solve this maze. Every wrong turn is a micro-decision. Save this for the next quiet afternoon. More puzzles like this in JoyMaze — free on iOS & Android.
pinterest.rawCaption: Most 6-year-olds take 4 tries to solve this maze. Every wrong turn is a micro-decision. Save this for the next quiet afternoon. More puzzles like this in JoyMaze — free on iOS & Android.

instagram.text:
You put the maze in front of them without a word. Twenty minutes later, the pencil stops. They look up — the first time they've noticed you watching. That's the moment a maze becomes more than a puzzle. How fast did yours solve it? Drop the time below. More like this in JoyMaze — link in bio.

youtube.text:
Watch what happens when a 6-year-old gets handed a maze with no instructions. They scan, they backtrack, they try again - that's spatial reasoning. For parents who choose activities with intention, more printable puzzles at joymaze.com
